Document:
# Aurelian Vox
[color=#ff0000]Warning![/color] [color=#ffa500]This species is not recommended for new players due to their fatal allergy to[/color] [color=#c4f5ff]Oxygen![/color]
Like regular Vox, the Aurelian variant also breathes nitrogen, and [color=#c4f5ff]Oxygen[/color] is [color=#6730ff]toxic[/color] to them. Unfortunately, space stations as well as space vessels tend to be full of oxygen. Due to this Vox must use an internal nitrogen supply at almost all times as well as possibly retrofit their vessels to help avoid fatal exposure.
Aurelian Vox always spawn wearing working nitrogen internals equipment. A spare breathing mask and an emergency nitrogen canister is provided in their survival box.
In addition to regular foods, Aurelian Vox can also eat various snack packaging, banana peels, eggshells, and raw meat without any ill effects. They also like to drink welding fuel.
Aurelian Vox slowly recover from low levels of [color=#6730ff]poison[/color] on their own, so long as they are careful not to exceed 20 damage total. This allows them to endure breathing station air for up to thirty seconds at a time without lasting damage, letting them quickly eat, drink, take oral medication, and so on. A thirty second oxygen exposure takes them two minutes to recover from. If their health does not seem to improve within a minute of oxygen exposure, they should seek medical attention.
[color=#0041a8]Dexalin[/color] as well as [color=#4da0bd]Dexalin Plus[/color] are [color=#c4f5ff]Oxygen[/color] based, making these chemicals extremely [color=#6730ff]toxic[/color] to Aurelian Vox.
[color=#a1e1ff]Nitrogen[/color] as well as [color=#5d2fde]Dylovene[/color] have an added benefit of restoring [color=#a1e1ff]Asphyxiation[/color] and [color=#6b0007]Bloodloss[/color] in Aurelian Vox.
The jagged claws of an Aurelian Vox make their unarmed attacks deal [color=#6b0007]Slash[/color] damage, continued attacks also tend to cause excessive [color=#6b0007]bleeding[/color] buildup.
The bodies of Aurelian Vox are covered in rigid [color=#ffff33]non-conductive[/color] plates, making them more durable than regular Vox, helping them handle pressure changes as well as tolerate slightly more punishment before collapsing. The stress of either of these situations slows them immensely, and in the event one is taken down, they succumb to their wounds much faster than most other species.
Their bodies are typically designed for quick, sudden actions, leaving them vulnerable to ambient heat buildup as well as being [color=#4da0bd]stunned[/color] through exhaustion; this flaw makes non-lethal methods one of the more effective ways to handle an Aurelian, or fire.
